Foundry Local is the missing enterprise-Windows entry point — Microsoft ships it as a ~20MB native library that bundles local AI into any app with hardware-matched WinML execution providers, and the obvious next step for Q2 2026 is to surface a TurboQuant flag in the Foundry Catalog. Apr 27 Microsoft–OpenAI partnership reset removed the cloud-exclusivity clause; Apr 28 OpenAI-on-Bedrock (limited preview) is the first cross-cloud frontier-model deployment in the post-exclusivity world; Apr 30 Codex CLI 0.128.0 model-provider-owned discovery + AWS Bedrock account-state exposure is the developer-side primitive; Apr 28 AWS Bedrock AgentCore Node.js + AgentCore CLI 14-region GA + Managed Harness 4-region preview is the orchestration substrate; Apr 30 Microsoft Foundry Agent Framework 1.0 GA + Toolbox preview is the parallel non-Bedrock path. The forcing function is enterprise procurement — once frontier models cross-deploy, the buyer's first comparison stops being "model" and starts being "model+SLA on cloud-of-record". The first SLA-standardization mover is most likely AWS Bedrock (because Bedrock has the broadest model selection and the strongest enterprise compliance posture); the first non-OpenAI mirror is most likely Anthropic given the Apr 27 Microsoft non-exclusive language explicitly applies to "OpenAI products" and creates white-space for parallel deals.
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: By H2 2026 the OpenAI-on-AWS-Bedrock + Microsoft-license-non-exclusive reset triggers a multi-cloud frontier-model SLA standardization wave

linked_predictions.prediction_summary: **"Attack surface against the MCP protocol itself" becomes the biggest enterprise AI adoption bottleneck by Q3 2026.** Nginx UI's **CVE-2026-33032 (MCPwn, CVSS 9.8)** — a structural defect where the `/mcp_message` endpoint, with its empty default IP allowlist, exposes 12 unauthenticated tool calls — was added to VulnCheck KEV after a 2-request real-world takeover in production. That tells us the MCP protocol itself needs to be redesigned as a web standard if it's going to scale. By Q3 2026, OWASP MCP Top 10 will be officially published, **MCP-over-mTLS / standardized OAuth scope specs by Anthropic / Microsoft / Google / Cisco / Cloudflare** will become industry standard, and Keycard / Okta for AI Agents / Wiz / Levo.ai / Pillar Security will roll out "MCP Gateway / MCP Firewall" products through Q4. Enterprise compliance audits will start tracking "number of MCP-connected tools / endpoints / authorization policies" as a new audit category. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: Attack surface against the MCP protocol itself" becomes the biggest enterprise AI adoption bottleneck by Q3 2026.

The economic forcing function is the same Mag-7 capex tape the Apr 28 OpenAI-miss WSJ report just cracked: every memory-bandwidth-bound long-context workload that can move from FP16 to 3.5-bit cuts effective per-token cost ~5-6x. By Q4 2026 hyperscalers gate Bedrock/Azure/Vertex per-token pricing on opt-in compressed-cache tiers, and the hardware-cap-on-incremental-GPU-capex thesis from yesterday's report tightens via this lever rather than via fewer GPUs. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: By Q4 2026 KV-cache-compression dtype is exposed as a first-class deployment knob in every major inference stack

linked_predictions.prediction_summary: **By H2 2026 every major local-LLM ecosystem (vLLM, SGLang, TGI, Ollama, llama.cpp, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, Foundry Local) exposes a unified compressed-KV-cache deployment knob — TurboQuant + at least one alternative — and Foundry Local (Microsoft, GA Apr 9) ships native TurboQuant integration tied to Windows ML / WinML execution providers as a Windows-default optimization.** Apr 27-29 evidence: SGLang PR #21419 in-tree; SGLang #23134 fused-Triton-decode kernel rewrite hitting 93–105% bf16 throughput; vLLM v0.19.0 (Apr 2) torch 2.11 / Python 3.14 / async scheduler default + Model Runner V2; PolyKV (arxiv 2604.24971, Apr 27) shared asymmetric KV-pool for multi-agent inference; NVIDIA kvpress at Decoding-Press maturity; llama.cpp #20969 TurboQuant discussion thread active; Unsloth 2026.4.8 inference 20-30% faster. Foundry Local is the missing enterprise-Windows entry point — Microsoft ships it as a ~20MB native library that bundles local AI into any app with hardware-matched WinML execution providers, and the obvious next step for Q2 2026 is to surface a TurboQuant flag in the Foundry Catalog. The economic forcing function is the same Mag 7 capex tape: every memory-bandwidth-bound long-context workload that moves from FP16 to 3.5-bit cuts effective per-token cost ~5-6x, and Foundry Local makes that switch invisible to end-developers.
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: By H2 2026 every major local-LLM ecosystem (vLLM, SGLang, TGI, Ollama, llama.cpp, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, Foundry Local) exposes a unified compressed-KV-cache deployment knob

linked_predictions.prediction_summary: **By Q4 2026 the Microsoft Agent 365 GA ($15/user, M365 E7) + Cisco DefenseClaw open-source (Mar 23) + CSAI Foundation MITRE-CNA (Apr 29) + AWS Bedrock AgentCore Managed Harness (Apr 28) stack converges into an enterprise agent-control-plane reference model — at least 2 of the Big-3 hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) ship policy-based MCP-server allow/block enforcement at the tenant level with audited tool-invocation logs as a default-on primitive; at least one independent control-plane vendor (Vanta, Zenity, OpenClawAI) publishes a Sec-Labels-style artifact attestation gate.** Forcing function is enterprise procurement: Microsoft just put a per-user price tag ($15/user/mo) on the agent-control-plane category, which sets the pricing-anchor that AWS + Google must respond to with comparable disclosures; the Apr 30 OpenAI Agents SDK + 7 sandbox providers pre-baked the runtime side, and the May 1 GA wires the policy side to an existing M365 admin surface (Entra/Purview/Intune/Defender). Once the May 1 GA's Defender asset-context mapping (devices + MCP servers + identities + cloud resources per agent) ships in June 2026 it becomes the de-facto schema; the path of least resistance is for AWS Bedrock AgentCore + Google Vertex Agent Garden to publish parallel schemas before Q4 to avoid losing the M365-installed-base anchor. CSAI's MITRE-CNA cadence will compress the disclosure side in parallel. In plain language: Microsoft put a price tag on controlling AI agents — other clouds will copy or lose customers. In plain language: In plain language:
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: Big-3 hyperscalers ship MCP-server policy enforcement as default by Q4 2026

In plain language: Microsoft just made AI agent IDs work across Microsoft, Amazon, and Google clouds — soon a public spec will lock that in. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

A US state (CA, NY, or TX likely) ships a statute by Q3 2026 mandating **agent-as-first-class-identity registration, MCP-call-chain logging, and per-action consent**, mirroring Cross App Access protocol semantics. EU AI Act Article 26 enforcement triggers in parallel. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: Public-opinion blowback against AI converts into binding agent-governance rules

The forcing function is the Cisco RSAC 2026 36%-prompt-injection-rate finding + ClawHavoc's 1,184 malicious skills + Apr 23-29 Google +32% IPI uptick; combined this is the same governance-gap arc that produced MITRE ATT&CK Mobile in 2017 and MITRE ATLAS in 2021. linked_predictions.prediction_summary: **"Attack surface against the MCP protocol itself" becomes the biggest enterprise AI adoption bottleneck by Q3 2026.** Nginx UI's **CVE-2026-33032 (MCPwn, CVSS 9.8)** — a structural defect where the `/mcp_message` endpoint, with its empty default IP allowlist, exposes 12 unauthenticated tool calls — was added to VulnCheck KEV after a 2-request real-world takeover in production. That tells us the MCP protocol itself needs to be redesigned as a web standard if it's going to scale. By Q3 2026, OWASP MCP Top 10 will be officially published, **MCP-over-mTLS / standardized OAuth scope specs by Anthropic / Microsoft / Google / Cisco / Cloudflare** will become industry standard, and Keycard / Okta for AI Agents / Wiz / Levo.ai / Pillar Security will roll out "MCP Gateway / MCP Firewall" products through Q4. Enterprise compliance audits will start tracking "number of MCP-connected tools / endpoints / authorization policies" as a new audit category. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: Attack surface against the MCP protocol itself" becomes the biggest enterprise AI adoption bottleneck by Q3 2026.

By Q3 2026, CVSS 8.0+ LLM-integration CVEs will be a Patch Tuesday regular. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: Indirect prompt injection becomes a top CVE category.

linked_predictions.prediction_summary: **The "1M context as default" open-weight era arrives in full by Q3 2026.** DeepSeek V4's "1M context designed in, not bolted on," Qwen3.6-27B's 262K → 1M extension, Claude Opus 4.7's 1M context (Bedrock), and GPT-5.5's long-context strengthening all point the same direction. By Q3 2026, "native 1M context" becomes the floor for open weights, and existing 32K-128K models drop to legacy. RAG pipelines flip from "search → context-insert" to "dump everything in + dynamic prune," and vector DB vendors are pushed to redefine themselves as "long-context cache optimization layers." linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: The "1M context as default" open-weight era arrives in full by Q3 2026.

linked_predictions.prediction_summary: **"27B Dense + 1M context" becomes the de facto standard for enterprise local LLMs in H2 2026.** Qwen3.6-27B demonstrates that the package — no MoE routing complexity, runs on a single RTX 4090, 1M context, Apache 2.0 — reaches flagship-grade coding ability. By Q3 2026, Mistral / Meta / DeepSeek / Google Gemma will each ship 20-30B Dense × 1M context × Apache/Llama-style models. The differentiators for 70B+ MoE narrow to multimodal understanding, tool breadth, and long-horizon reasoning. On-prem inference's default VRAM requirement shifts down from RTX 6000 48GB / H100 80GB to **RTX 4090 24GB / Apple M4 Max 64GB**. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: 27B Dense + 1M context" becomes the de facto standard for enterprise local LLMs in H2 2026.

By Q4 2026 hyperscalers gate Bedrock/Azure/Vertex per-token pricing on opt-in compressed-cache tiers, and the hardware-cap-on-incremental-GPU-capex thesis from yesterday's report tightens via this lever rather than via fewer GPUs. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: By Q4 2026 KV-cache-compression dtype is exposed as a first-class deployment knob in every major inference stack

linked_predictions.prediction_summary: **By H2 2026 every major local-LLM ecosystem (vLLM, SGLang, TGI, Ollama, llama.cpp, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, Foundry Local) exposes a unified compressed-KV-cache deployment knob — TurboQuant + at least one alternative — and Foundry Local (Microsoft, GA Apr 9) ships native TurboQuant integration tied to Windows ML / WinML execution providers as a Windows-default optimization.** Apr 27-29 evidence: SGLang PR #21419 in-tree; SGLang #23134 fused-Triton-decode kernel rewrite hitting 93–105% bf16 throughput; vLLM v0.19.0 (Apr 2) torch 2.11 / Python 3.14 / async scheduler default + Model Runner V2; PolyKV (arxiv 2604.24971, Apr 27) shared asymmetric KV-pool for multi-agent inference; NVIDIA kvpress at Decoding-Press maturity; llama.cpp #20969 TurboQuant discussion thread active; Unsloth 2026.4.8 inference 20-30% faster. Foundry Local is the missing enterprise-Windows entry point — Microsoft ships it as a ~20MB native library that bundles local AI into any app with hardware-matched WinML execution providers, and the obvious next step for Q2 2026 is to surface a TurboQuant flag in the Foundry Catalog. The economic forcing function is the same Mag 7 capex tape: every memory-bandwidth-bound long-context workload that moves from FP16 to 3.5-bit cuts effective per-token cost ~5-6x, and Foundry Local makes that switch invisible to end-developers.
linked_predictions.prediction_short_label: By H2 2026 every major local-LLM ecosystem (vLLM, SGLang, TGI, Ollama, llama.cpp, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, Foundry Local) exposes a unified compressed-KV-cache deployment knob
